text:
To determine the total cost for Michael to replace the carpet in his bedroom, we need to calculate the costs associated with the new carpet, padding, old carpet removal, and installation step-by-step.

First, let's calculate the area of the bedroom:
\[ \text{Area} = 18 \text{ feet} \times 12 \text{ feet} = 216 \text{ square feet} \]

### Cost of the New Carpet
The cost of the new carpet is $12 per square foot:
\[ \text{Cost of new carpet} = 216 \text{ square feet} \times 12 \text{ dollars/square foot} = 2592 \text{ dollars} \]

### Cost of the Padding
The cost of the padding is $2 per square foot:
\[ \text{Cost of padding} = 216 \text{ square feet} \times 2 \text{ dollars/square foot} = 432 \text{ dollars} \]

### Cost of Removing the Old Carpet
The cost of removing the old carpet is $4 per square foot:
\[ \text{Cost of removing old carpet} = 216 \text{ square feet} \times 4 \text{ dollars/square foot} = 864 \text{ dollars} \]

### Cost of Installation
The cost of installation is $34 per square foot:
\[ \text{Cost of installation} = 216 \text{ square feet} \times 34 \text{ dollars/square foot} = 7344 \text{ dollars} \]

### Total Cost
Now we sum up all the costs:
\[ \text{Total cost} = \text{Cost of new carpet} + \text{Cost of padding} + \text{Cost of removing old carpet} + \text{Cost of installation} \]
\[ \text{Total cost} = 2592 \text{ dollars} + 432 \text{ dollars} + 864 \text{ dollars} + 7344 \text{ dollars} \]
\[ \text{Total cost} = 11232 \text{ dollars} \]

Therefore, the total cost for Michael to replace the carpet in his bedroom is \(\boxed{11232}\) dollars.
